title: Philly Cheesesteak Pasta Skillet
description: EveryPlate fan favorite — all the flavors of a classic Philly cheesesteak in a one-pan pasta skillet. Thinly sliced beef, sautéed peppers and onions, and a creamy provolone sauce tossed with penne. Pure comfort food in 30 minutes.

ingredients.item: Shaved or thinly sliced beef (ribeye or sirloin)

instructions: Cook penne in salted boiling water until al dente. Drain, reserving ½ cup pasta water.
Slice bell pepper and onion into thin strips. Slice mushrooms. Mince garlic.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add beef in a single layer; cook undisturbed 2 minutes, then stir and cook 1–2 more minutes until browned. Season with salt and pepper. Remove to a plate.
In the same skillet, heat remaining olive oil over medium-high heat. Add onion and bell pepper; cook 5 minutes until softened. Add mushrooms and cook 3 more minutes. Add garlic; cook 1 minute.
Add Worcestershire sauce and beef broth. Scrape up any browned bits. Bring to a simmer.
Add cream cheese and stir until melted and incorporated into the sauce.
Add drained pasta and cooked beef back to the skillet. Toss to combine, adding pasta water as needed.
Lay provolone slices over the top. Cover with a lid for 2 minutes until cheese melts. Serve directly from the pan.
tips: Shaved beef from the butcher counter is ideal; you can also freeze a sirloin steak for 20 minutes and slice it paper-thin yourself.
Don't overcook the beef — it's thin and only needs 3–4 minutes total. It will continue cooking in the sauce.
American cheese or Cheez Whiz can substitute for provolone for a more authentic Philly flavor.
Add a pinch of cayenne for a little heat.
